response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong polyglot experience and demonstrated systems thinking, including low-latency work at Sociomantic and GIS/geospatial work via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ (or Rust) and navigation/routing pipeline experience, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure (~6 months at NI a decade ago) with no Rust and no routing-tile or large-scale map pipeline work. The geospatial overlap and proven ability to learn new stacks make this a borderline 'maybe' rather than a clear yes.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS background via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, distributed systems exposure
concerns: Minimal recent modern C++ experience; no Rust on resume
No direct navigation/routing pipeline or map-tile production experience
Recent work centered on TypeScript/Node web stack, not systems-level data pipelines
